questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Biodiversity is the variety of different species of plants, animals, and microorganisms that live in an ecosystem.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It is essential for the functioning of an ecosystem.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because biodiversity indeed includes the variety of different species of plants, animals, and microorganisms that live in an ecosystem and it is essential for the functioning of an ecosystem.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): In-situ conservation involves the protection of ecosystems and natural habitats in their natural locations.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This method helps in maintaining the natural balance of the ecosystem.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because in-situ conservation indeed involves the protection of ecosystems and natural habitats in their natural locations and this method helps in maintaining the natural balance of the ecosystem.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Ex-situ conservation involves the protection of plants and animals outside their natural habitats.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This method helps in conserving endangered species.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because ex-situ conservation indeed involves the protection of plants and animals outside their natural habitats and this method helps in conserving endangered species.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Habitat fragmentation is a major threat to biodiversity.

(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Endangered species are those species that are likely to become extinct in near future.

(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Invasive species are those species that are introduced into an ecosystem from outside.

(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
